questions.question: Find the HCF of 36 and 84 using the prime factorization method.
The HCF of two numbers is 6 and their LCM is 36. If one number is 12, find the other number.
questions.answer: Product of numbers = HCF × LCM. 12 × x = 6 × 36. x = 216 / 12 = 18.

questions.question: What is the smallest number which when divided by 12, 16, and 24 leaves no remainder?
questions.answer: LCM of 12, 16, and 24 is 48.

questions.question: Find the HCF of 108 and 144.
Two bells toll at intervals of 15 and 20 minutes respectively. If they toll together at 8:00 AM, at what time will they toll together again?
questions.answer: LCM of 15 and 20 is 60 minutes. They will toll together at 9:00 AM.

questions.question: Find the greatest number that can exactly divide 42, 70, and 98.
questions.answer: HCF of 42, 70, and 98 is 14.

questions.question: If the LCM of two co-prime numbers is 117, what is their HCF?
questions.answer: The HCF of any two co-prime numbers is 1.

questions.question: Find the LCM of 16, 24, and 40 using the division method.

Three tankers contain 403 liters, 434 liters, and 465 liters of diesel respectively. Find the maximum capacity of a container that can measure the diesel of all three tankers in exact number of times.
questions.answer: HCF of 403, 434, and 465 is 31 liters.

questions.question: Are the numbers 18 and 35 co-prime? Justify your answer.
questions.answer: Factors of 18: 1, 2, 3, 6, 9, 18. Factors of 35: 1, 5, 7, 35. Only common factor is 1, so they are co-prime.

questions.question: Find the smallest 4-digit number which is divisible by 18, 24, and 32.
questions.answer: LCM of 18, 24, 32 is 288. Multiples of 288 are 288, 576, 864, 1152. Smallest 4-digit is 1152.

questions.question: Can two numbers have 15 as their HCF and 110 as their LCM? Give reason.
questions.answer: No, because the HCF must always be a factor of the LCM, and 110 is not divisible by 15.

questions.question: Find the prime factorization of 980.
The product of two numbers is 2160 and their HCF is 12. Find their LCM.
questions.answer: LCM = Product / HCF = 2160 / 12 = 180.

questions.question: What is the HCF of 75, 60, and 105?
Find the LCM of 20, 25, and 30.
Find the smallest number which when divided by 6, 15, and 18 leaves a remainder 5 in each case.
questions.answer: LCM of 6, 15, 18 is 90. Required number = 90 + 5 = 95.

questions.question: Explain the difference between a factor and a multiple.
questions.answer: A factor divides a number exactly, while a multiple is a product of that number and an integer.

questions.question: Find the HCF of two consecutive even numbers.
questions.answer: The HCF of any two consecutive even numbers is always 2.

questions.question: Find the LCM of 12 and 48. What do you observe?
questions.answer: LCM is 48. Observation: If one number is a factor of another, the larger number is their LCM.

questions.question: Express 144 as a product of powers of its prime factors.
What is the HCF of 17 and 31?
questions.answer: 1 (since both are prime).

questions.question: Find the LCM of 9 and 4. Are they co-prime?
questions.answer: LCM is 36. Yes, they are co-prime as HCF is 1.

questions.question: Find the largest number that divides 60 and 75 leaving no remainder.
questions.answer: HCF of 60 and 75 is 15.

questions.question: Determine the prime factorization of 20570.
